findings.implication: Scientific Abstract: Clearly outlines problem, aims, methods, and impact, supporting pr.1.

Plain English Summary of Research: Clearly defines the problem (SMA), objectives, methods (3 parts), beneficiaries, and impact in accessible language, strongly supporting pr.1.
Plain English Summary of Research: Identifies a specific knowledge gap regarding airway dynamics during cough-assist use, supporting pr.2.

Plain English Summary of Research: Describes explicit methods including endoscopy, motion capture with stickers, and visual scales, supporting pr.4.
Plain English Summary of Research: Highlights direct patient benefits (reduced infections, hospital visits) and NHS cost savings, strongly supporting pr.5.
Plain English Summary of Research: States that children and parents will guide information sharing, indicating engagement with the community, supporting pr.6.
Plain English Summary of Research: Mentions adaptive marker counts based on child size and assessing tolerance/comfort, supporting inclusive design for pr.9.
Plain English Summary of Research: Plans to include children and parents in guiding dissemination, reflecting integration of lived experience, supporting wp.1.
Plain English Summary of Research: Details plans to share findings via meetings, conferences, and journals to maximize public benefit, supporting wp.3.
Plain English Summary of Research: Focuses on children with Spinal Muscular Atrophy, a rare disease affecting a specific underserved group, supporting wp.4.
Plain English Summary of Research: Adapts methods to child size and assesses comfort/tolerance, demonstrating inclusivity across the research lifecycle, supporting wp.5.

Patient & Public Involvement: Confirms the involvement group includes members from diverse socio-economic and ethnic backgrounds, supporting wp.4.
Patient & Public Involvement: Demonstrates inclusivity across the lifecycle from initial planning and plain English summaries to ongoing advisory roles and dissemination, supporting wp.5.
